title: ParseContextStdioDriftSurfaced payload
description: Emitted by the parse_context handler whenever it evaluates the stdio-drift surface — whether or not it ended up surfacing a Candidate. Lets T10's drift-fire-rate measurement reason about both fire and suppress paths from one event stream. Chain parse-context-lean-orienting T9.

head_sha.description: The git HEAD SHA at the time of the parse_context call, or empty when `git rev-parse HEAD` was unreachable. Used by T10's drift-rate dashboards to correlate fires against commit cadence.

stdio_sha.description: The stdio process's reported gitSHA (typically the toolkit-server binary's compile-time main.gitSHA). Empty when the snapshot couldn't observe a per-PID SHA — the drift signal then rests on fd_deleted alone.

drift_kind.description: The drift classification. 'none' fires when parse_context evaluated drift and found none — the event still emits so T10 can compute a fire-rate denominator.

bootstrap_path.description: True when this is the first parse_context call in the MCP session and the bootstrap surface fired regardless of T5's directive intent. The session-scoped first-fire signal lives in HandlerDeps.DriftFireTracker.

suppressed_by_recent_fire.description: True when drift was detected AND a Candidate would have surfaced, but the per-session fire counter exceeded its threshold (default 3) so the substrate suppressed the surface. The event still emits so T10's measurement can distinguish 'surfaced' from 'detected-but-suppressed'.
